subtitle: The easternmost of the five D-Day beaches, where the British 3rd Infantry Division landed on 6 June 1944 to link up with the airborne troops at the Orne bridges and take Caen — now an 8-kilometer ferry-port and resort seafront across Ouistreham, Colleville-Montgomery, and Hermanville-sur-Mer.
spike_statement: The easternmost D-Day beach, where the British 3rd Division landed to reach the Pegasus Bridge gliders and take Caen — Caen held for a month.
